name: Sigiriya Rock Fortress
description: Ancient rock fortress and UNESCO World Heritage Site rising 200m above the surrounding plains. Features 5th-century frescoes, mirror wall, and lion paws gateway. One of Sri Lanka's most iconic landmarks.
best_time_to_visit: Early morning 7:00 AM to avoid heat and crowds

name: Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic (Sri Dalada Maligawa)
description: Sri Lanka's most sacred Buddhist temple housing the relic of the tooth of the Buddha. A UNESCO World Heritage Site and center of Esala Perahera festival. Dress code strictly required.
best_time_to_visit: Morning puja at 5:30 AM or during Esala Perahera (July/August)

name: Pedro Tea Estate
description: One of Sri Lanka's premier high-grown tea plantations offering factory tours and tea tasting experiences. Located in the picturesque hills of Nuwara Eliya at an elevation of 1,800m.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for factory tour when production is active

name: Mlesna Tea Centre
description: Premium Ceylon tea boutique offering an extensive range of finest Ceylon teas, gift packs, and tea accessories. Perfect for high-end tea gifting and souvenir shopping.
best_time_to_visit: Any time; weekday mornings for personal attention

name: New Ranweli Spice Garden
description: Traditional spice garden and herbal products center offering guided tours through cinnamon, cardamom, pepper, nutmeg, and medicinal herb plantations. Features Ayurvedic treatments and herbal product sales.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for guided tours

name: Yala National Park
description: Sri Lanka's most visited and second largest national park, famous for the world's highest density of leopards. Also home to elephants, sloth bears, crocodiles, and 215+ bird species.
best_time_to_visit: February to July for best wildlife sightings

name: Mirissa Whale Watching
description: World-class whale watching destination offering sightings of blue whales, sperm whales, and spinner dolphins. Mirissa is considered one of the best whale watching spots in the world.
best_time_to_visit: December to April for best whale sightings

name: Arugam Bay Surf School
description: World-renowned surf destination and one of Asia's top surfing spots. Offers surf lessons for beginners and advanced surfers. The bay hosts international surf competitions.
best_time_to_visit: May to September for best surf conditions

name: Siddhalepa Ayurveda Resort
description: Sri Lanka's most prestigious Ayurvedic resort chain offering authentic treatments, herbal therapies, and wellness programs. Certified by the Ministry of Health with qualified Ayurvedic physicians.
best_time_to_visit: Year-round; book treatments in advance

name: Galle Fort
description: 16th-century Dutch colonial fort and UNESCO World Heritage Site. Features intact ramparts, cobblestone streets, colonial architecture, boutique shops, and restaurants within the fort walls.
best_time_to_visit: Early morning or sunset for photography; December-March for best weather

name: Polonnaruwa Ancient City
description: UNESCO World Heritage ancient capital featuring remarkable stone sculptures, Buddhist temples, royal palaces, and the famous Gal Vihara rock temple. The medieval capital of Sri Lanka.
best_time_to_visit: Early morning to avoid heat; May-October dry season

name: Anuradhapura Sacred City
description: Sri Lanka's first ancient capital and UNESCO World Heritage Site with stupas, ancient pools, and the sacred Bodhi Tree (oldest documented tree in the world). A living pilgrimage city.
best_time_to_visit: Early morning for pilgrimage atmosphere; November-April dry season

name: Adams Peak (Sri Pada)
description: Sacred conical mountain with a summit footprint revered by Buddhists, Hindus, Muslims, and Christians. The pilgrimage season climb offers spectacular sunrise views from 2,243m altitude.
opening_hours: Pilgrimage season: December to May (night climbs)
best_time_to_visit: December to May for pilgrimage season; full moon nights most crowded

name: Horton Plains National Park
description: UNESCO World Heritage montane grassland plateau at 2,100m. Features the dramatic World's End cliff drop and Baker's Falls waterfall. Home to sambar deer, leopards, and rare birds.
best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6-9 AM) to avoid mist; January-April dry season

name: Sinharaja Forest Reserve
description: Sri Lanka's last remaining lowland tropical rainforest and UNESCO World Heritage Site. Extraordinary biodiversity including 60% endemic trees, leopards, and rare purple-faced langurs.
best_time_to_visit: August-September and January-February (drier periods)

name: Ella Rock Hike
description: One of Sri Lanka's most rewarding hikes offering panoramic views over Ella Gap, tea plantations, and surrounding valleys. The 4km trail passes through rice paddies, jungle, and tea estates.
best_time_to_visit: Early morning for clear views; May-September dry season

name: Nine Arches Bridge (Demodara)
description: Iconic colonial-era railway viaduct bridge built entirely of stone and brick without steel, standing 24m high with nine elegant arches. One of Sri Lanka's most photographed locations.
best_time_to_visit: Train passes at approximately 9:15 AM and 3:00 PM daily

name: Colombo National Museum
description: The largest museum in Sri Lanka housing a vast collection of royal regalia, ancient sculptures, masks, traditional handicrafts, and natural history exhibits spanning 2,500 years of Sri Lankan civilization.
best_time_to_visit: Weekday mornings for fewer crowds

name: Kandy Lake & Royal Palace Area
description: Scenic artificial lake built in 1807 by the last Kandyan king, surrounded by the royal palace, cloud wall, and the sacred tooth temple. A tranquil heart of the ancient Kandyan Kingdom.
best_time_to_visit: Early morning or evening for lakeside walks

name: Dambulla Cave Temple
description: The largest and best-preserved cave temple complex in Sri Lanka, a UNESCO World Heritage Site with 153 statues of Buddha, 3 statues of Sri Lankan kings, and 4 statues of gods across five caves.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for cooler temperatures and better light

name: Udawalawe National Park
description: Famous for large herds of wild Sri Lankan elephants. One of the best places in Asia to see wild elephants in their natural habitat, with over 600 resident elephants.
best_time_to_visit: May-September for dry season and best elephant viewing

name: Elephant Transit Home Udawalawe
description: Government-run orphanage rehabilitating orphaned baby elephants for reintroduction to the wild. Feeding time (daily at 9 AM, noon, 3 PM, 6 PM) draws visitors from around the world.

name: Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage
description: World's largest captive elephant herd with 80+ elephants. Visitors can observe feeding and bathing in the Maha Oya River. One of Sri Lanka's most visited tourist attractions.
best_time_to_visit: Bathing times: 10:00 AM and 2:00 PM

name: Minneriya National Park
description: Home to The Gathering – one of the largest congregation of Asian elephants in the world (July-September). Up to 300 elephants gather at Minneriya Tank when surrounding areas dry up.
best_time_to_visit: July-September for The Gathering elephant congregation

name: Wilpattu National Park
description: Sri Lanka's largest national park, known for its unique natural lakes (villus) and the highest density of leopards after Yala. Recently reopened after decades and still offers uncrowded safari experiences.
best_time_to_visit: February to October dry season

name: Ritigala Forest Monastery
description: Ancient forest monastery ruins hidden deep in a designated strict nature reserve. A mysterious atmospheric site rarely visited, featuring unique double-platform meditation walks built by forest-dwelling monks.
best_time_to_visit: Early morning; May-September dry season

name: Negombo Fish Market
description: One of Sri Lanka's most vibrant morning fish markets where fishermen bring in fresh catch from the Indian Ocean. Authentic local experience offering insight into the fishing community.
best_time_to_visit: Early morning 5:00-7:00 AM when boats return

name: Hela Bojun Authentic Sri Lankan Food Hall
description: Government-backed women-run food court serving authentic traditional Sri Lankan home-cooked food at very affordable prices. The best place in Colombo to try genuine local cuisine.
best_time_to_visit: Lunchtime for freshest food variety

name: Palmyrah Restaurant Jaffna
description: Iconic Jaffna restaurant serving authentic Northern Tamil cuisine including Jaffna crab curry, mutton rolls, and traditional Jaffna rice and curry. A culinary institution since 1956.
best_time_to_visit: Lunch or early evening

name: Ministry of Crab Colombo
description: Award-winning restaurant in a restored 1904 Dutch Hospital celebrating Sri Lanka's lagoon crabs and seafood. Founded by cricket legends Kumar Sangakkara and Mahela Jayawardena. Asia's 50 Best Restaurants listed.
best_time_to_visit: Dinner with advance reservation

name: Jaffna Lagoon Fishing Tour
description: Traditional fishing experience with local Tamil fishermen on Jaffna Lagoon. Learn ancient fishing techniques, cast nets, and enjoy fresh catch cooked on the boat or at the village.
best_time_to_visit: Early morning at sunrise for best experience

name: Weligama Surf School
description: Sri Lanka's most beginner-friendly surf spot with a long sandy beach and gentle rolling waves. Multiple surf schools offer lessons for complete beginners. Stilt fishermen add to the picturesque backdrop.
best_time_to_visit: November to April for best surf conditions

name: Ratnapura Gem Museum & Market
description: Sri Lanka is the world's gem capital and Ratnapura is its gem city. The National Gem & Jewellery Authority museum displays world-famous blue sapphires, rubies, and cat's eyes. Adjacent market offers genuine gems.
best_time_to_visit: Weekday mornings for gem trading activity

name: Colombo City Cooking Class
description: Hands-on Sri Lankan cooking classes in local homes or boutique venues. Learn to prepare rice and curry, hoppers, kottu, and sambol. Includes market visit, cooking, and dining together.
best_time_to_visit: Any time; book 24 hours in advance

name: Kandy Cultural Village Tour
description: Immersive village walking tour in the Kandy highlands experiencing traditional paddy farming, ayurvedic village life, batik weaving, and rural Sri Lankan family hospitality. Authentic cultural exchange.
best_time_to_visit: Morning departure; year-round

name: Trincomalee Koneswaram Temple
description: Ancient Hindu temple perched dramatically on Swami Rock overlooking the Indian Ocean and Trincomalee harbour. One of the Pancha Ishwarams (five abodes of Shiva) in Sri Lanka.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for sunrise over the harbour; July-August Ther festival

name: Pigeon Island National Park
description: Sri Lanka's only coral reef national park located 1km offshore. Crystal clear waters with vibrant coral gardens, reef sharks, turtles, and rainbow fish. One of Asia's best snorkeling spots.
best_time_to_visit: April-September for best visibility and calmest water

name: Nilaveli Beach
description: One of Sri Lanka's most pristine and longest beaches on the East coast with turquoise calm waters. Ideal for swimming, snorkeling, and dolphin watching in season.
best_time_to_visit: April to September for calm seas

name: Unawatuna Beach
description: Beautiful horseshoe-shaped bay with calm turquoise waters, white sand, and a vibrant beach atmosphere. Popular for swimming, snorkeling, and beach cafés. Close proximity to Galle Fort.
best_time_to_visit: November to April for calm waters and good visibility

name: Mirissa Beach
description: Crescent-shaped beach with swaying coconut palms, calm waters, and a relaxed vibe. Famous for whale watching, stilt fishermen, and beautiful sunsets. One of Sri Lanka's trendiest beach destinations.
best_time_to_visit: December to April for whale season and best beach weather

name: Kandy Esala Perahera (Seasonal Event)
description: Sri Lanka's most spectacular cultural procession held annually in July-August over 10 days. Features decorated elephants, Kandyan dancers, fire-breathers, and drummers parading through Kandy. A UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage.
best_time_to_visit: Last 3 nights for the grandest procession

name: Colombo Pettah Market
description: Colombo's bustling historic bazaar district with specialized streets for electronics, textiles, spices, fruits, and everyday goods. A sensory overload of South Asian market culture.
best_time_to_visit: Weekday mornings for authentic market activity

name: Hikkaduwa Coral Reef Snorkeling
description: Protected coral sanctuary in Hikkaduwa offering excellent snorkeling and diving in shallow reefs with sea turtles, tropical fish, and coral gardens. A popular marine national park.
best_time_to_visit: November to April for best visibility

name: Colombo Fort & Dutch Hospital Shopping Precinct
description: Restored 400-year-old Dutch Hospital turned premium shopping and dining precinct in Colombo Fort. Upscale boutiques, fine dining, cafés, and cocktail bars in colonial architecture.
best_time_to_visit: Evening for dining and drinks

name: Ambuluwawa Tower
description: Unique multi-religion tower and biodiversity complex offering 360-degree panoramic views of mountains and valleys. Features Buddhist, Hindu, Christian, and Muslim religious structures on one hilltop.
best_time_to_visit: Clear morning for panoramic views

name: Nuwara Eliya Post Office (Photography Spot)
description: Iconic red-brick colonial post office in Little England - the most photographed colonial building in Sri Lanka. Surrounded by manicured gardens, a lake, and English-style architecture.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for best light; April for Sinhala New Year races

name: BOC ATM Colombo Fort
description: Bank of Ceylon main branch and ATM in Colombo Fort – central banking hub with reliable foreign card acceptance (Visa, Mastercard, UnionPay) and currency exchange services. 24-hour ATM.
best_time_to_visit: Weekday banking hours for currency exchange

name: People's Bank Currency Exchange Kandy
description: People's Bank main branch in Kandy city centre offering competitive exchange rates for major currencies. Reliable ATM services with Visa, Mastercard, and international network acceptance.
best_time_to_visit: Any time for ATM; banking hours for exchange

name: Dialog Axiata Service Centre Colombo
description: Sri Lanka's largest mobile network operator. Tourist SIM cards available with 4G/5G data packages, WhatsApp bundles, and international calling. Available at airport and all major outlets.
best_time_to_visit: On arrival at Bandaranaike Airport for immediate connectivity

name: Mobitel Tourist SIM Outlet Katunayake Airport
description: Sri Lanka Telecom's Mobitel SIM outlet at Bandaranaike International Airport offering tourist SIM cards with data packages and island-wide 4G coverage immediately on arrival.
best_time_to_visit: On arrival at airport

name: National Hospital Colombo
description: Sri Lanka's largest and most advanced government hospital with 24-hour emergency services, specialist doctors, and modern ICU facilities. Free emergency treatment for all persons.
best_time_to_visit: Emergency - 24 hours; OPD early morning

name: Asiri Central Hospital Colombo
description: Premium private hospital with 24-hour emergency services, international patient department, and English-speaking staff. Accepts international health insurance and travel insurance.
best_time_to_visit: Emergency - 24 hours

name: Colombo Fort Railway Station
description: Sri Lanka's main railway hub and departure point for iconic scenic train journeys to Kandy and Ella. Historical colonial-era station serving all major routes across the island.
best_time_to_visit: Book Kandy/Ella train tickets at least 2 days in advance

name: Kandy Railway Station
description: Kandy's main railway station and starting point for the celebrated Kandy-Ella scenic train journey, one of the world's most beautiful train routes through tea country.
best_time_to_visit: Book scenic Ella train (Observation Car) tickets in advance

name: Colombo Central Bus Stand (Pettah)
description: Sri Lanka's largest bus terminal and intercity bus hub with connections to all major cities, tourist destinations, and provincial towns across Sri Lanka. Air-conditioned express services available.
best_time_to_visit: Early morning for direct intercity buses

name: Nuwara Eliya Gregory Lake
description: Picturesque artificial lake in the heart of Nuwara Eliya, Sri Lanka's Little England. Surrounded by rose gardens, colonial bungalows, and manicured parks with a cool misty mountain climate.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for mist photography; April for racing season

name: Bundala National Park (Birding)
description: UNESCO Biosphere Reserve and internationally recognized bird sanctuary hosting over 200 bird species including lesser flamingos, painted storks, and migratory waders from Siberia.
best_time_to_visit: September to March for migratory birds; flamingos October-March

name: Rekawa Beach Turtle Watching
description: One of Asia's most important sea turtle nesting beaches where all five sea turtle species nest. Guided night tours allow visitors to witness nesting and hatchling release in a conservation-focused setting.
best_time_to_visit: February to August for nesting; March-April for hatchlings

name: Jaffna Fort
description: One of the largest remaining Dutch forts in Asia, built in 1658. Massive star-shaped fortification with moat, watchtowers, and Dutch-period architecture. Partially restored with ocean-facing ramparts.
best_time_to_visit: Morning; May-September dry season

name: Mannar Island & Adam's Bridge Viewpoint
description: Remote island connected by causeway, home to ancient baobab trees, massive bird migration, and the start of the legendary Rama's Bridge (Adam's Bridge) connecting Sri Lanka to India.
best_time_to_visit: October-March for bird migration; September-October for flamingos

name: Tangalle Medaketiya Beach
description: Secluded, uncrowded beach with natural rock formations, calm lagoon for swimming, and pristine white sands. One of Sri Lanka's best-kept beach secrets, favored by independent travelers.
best_time_to_visit: December to April for calm seas

name: Kitulgala White Water Rafting
description: Sri Lanka's best white water rafting destination on the Kelani River. Grade 2-4 rapids through lush jungle. Also famous as the filming location for the Bridge on the River Kwai and for canyoning.
best_time_to_visit: May-December (higher water levels); Year-round rafting available

name: Handunugoda Tea Estate (Virgin White Tea)
description: Home of the world-famous Virgin White Tea (one of the world's most expensive teas). Estate tours include tea factory visit, tea tasting, and insights into luxury tea production in southern Sri Lanka.
best_time_to_visit: Morning; call ahead to confirm tour availability

name: Lipton's Seat Tea Plantation Viewpoint
description: Iconic misty hilltop where Sir Thomas Lipton surveyed his tea empire. Surrounded by endless Haputale tea estates rolling into the mist. One of Sri Lanka's most dramatic landscape photography spots.
best_time_to_visit: Clear mornings; July-September for least fog

name: Kanniyai Hot Springs
description: Seven natural hot spring wells with different water temperatures located near Trincomalee, revered as sacred by Hindus. Used for therapeutic bathing, believed to have healing properties.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for peaceful atmosphere

name: Bandarawela Ayurveda Health Resort
description: Established Ayurvedic resort in Uva highlands offering authentic panchakarma treatments, herbal steam baths, yoga, and vegetarian Ayurvedic cuisine in a serene mountain environment.
best_time_to_visit: Year-round; minimum 3-day residential package recommended

name: Colombo Gangaramaya Temple
description: One of Colombo's most important and eclectic Buddhist temples featuring an extraordinary museum of religious artifacts from around Asia. The annual Navam Perahera in February is spectacular.
best_time_to_visit: Morning or February for Navam Perahera

name: Lahugala Kitulana National Park
description: Small but ecologically significant national park famous for large elephant herds gathering at the Lahugala Tank. Also a superb birding destination with many waterbirds and raptors.
best_time_to_visit: August-September for elephant gatherings at the tank

name: Batik Sri Lanka Workshop Colombo
description: Traditional batik fabric art workshop where tourists learn the ancient wax-resist dyeing technique. Create your own sarong or fabric design using traditional patterns. Authentic handicraft experience.
best_time_to_visit: Morning sessions for best results

name: Mask Museum Ambalangoda
description: Legendary traditional Sri Lankan mask carving center in Ambalangoda – the home of Kolam and Sanni masks used in exorcism rituals and dance dramas. Museum with 300+ masks and workshop demonstrations.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for carving demonstrations

name: Poya Day Cultural Experience
description: Monthly full moon Poya day Buddhist experience visiting temples, observing all-white dressed devotees, participating in oil lamp lighting ceremonies, and witnessing the quieter, spiritual side of Sri Lanka.
best_time_to_visit: Full moon day (Poya Day) monthly

name: Gem & Jewellery Fair Beruwala
description: Beruwala is the heart of Sri Lanka's Moor (Muslim) trading community and hosts gem traders since Arab merchant times. Visit gem workshops, Moor architecture, and the oldest mosque in Sri Lanka.
best_time_to_visit: Monday and Thursday market days

name: Knuckles Mountain Range Trekking
description: UNESCO World Heritage wilderness zone with challenging mountain treks through diverse ecosystems, waterfalls, ancient villages, and cloud forests. Named after the knuckle-like appearance of the peaks.
opening_hours: Dawn to dusk (guided treks only recommended)
best_time_to_visit: January to April and August to September for best trekking

name: Kataragama Sacred Pilgrimage Site
description: One of Sri Lanka's most sacred multi-religious pilgrimage sites venerated by Buddhists, Hindus, and indigenous Veddah people. The July Esala Festival attracts hundreds of thousands with fire-walking ceremonies.
best_time_to_visit: July-August Esala Festival; daily evening fire-walking

name: Pasikuda Bay Beach
description: East coast beach with exceptional flat, shallow, crystal-clear water ideal for families and non-swimmers. One of the calmest and most pristine beaches in Asia, emerging as a luxury resort destination.
best_time_to_visit: April to September for calm east coast seas

name: Batticaloa Singing Fish Lagoon
description: Mysterious natural phenomenon where the Batticaloa Lagoon produces musical singing sounds on full moon nights, believed to be caused by catfish. A unique natural curiosity attracting curious travelers.
best_time_to_visit: Full moon nights; calm conditions

name: Galle International Cricket Stadium
description: One of the world's most beautiful cricket grounds, located alongside the 16th-century Galle Fort. Watching a Test match here is an unforgettable experience with Dutch ramparts as backdrop.
best_time_to_visit: During scheduled international matches

name: Sri Lanka Cultural Show (Kandy)
description: Nightly traditional cultural dance performance featuring Kandyan dancing, low country dancing, fire walking, and acrobatic fire shows. Performed at dedicated cultural halls near the Tooth Temple.
best_time_to_visit: Any evening; arrive 30 minutes early for front row

name: Herbal Village Matale
description: Traditional Ayurvedic village offering extensive herbal garden tours, body massage treatments, herbal steam baths, and medicinal plant identification. Certified Ayurvedic practitioners on site.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for full treatment sessions

name: Kalpitiya Dolphin & Whale Watching
description: Northwestern coast spot offering spectacular encounters with massive pods of spinner dolphins (500-1000+) and occasional blue whales. Also a world-class kitesurfing and windsurfing destination.
best_time_to_visit: November to April for dolphins; December-March for kite surfing

name: Kuragala Ancient Buddhist Monastery
description: Remote ancient cave monastery perched on a dramatic rocky outcrop featuring 2,000-year-old meditation caves, Brahmi inscriptions, and coexisting Sufi shrine. An extraordinary hidden gem of Sri Lanka.
best_time_to_visit: Early morning; dry season November to March

name: Dutch Canal Kayaking (Negombo)
description: Historic 17th-century Dutch canal network stretching 120km along the west coast. Kayak through the mangroves, fishing villages, and Buddhist temples observing daily Sri Lankan coastal life from the water.
best_time_to_visit: Early morning for calmest water and bird life

name: Victoria Park Nuwara Eliya (Bird Watching)
description: Manicured colonial-era park in the center of Nuwara Eliya attracting migrant birds from India in April and October. Home to the Kashmir flycatcher, pied thrush, and Indian pitta during migration.
best_time_to_visit: April and October for migrant bird species

name: Mangrove Forest Kayaking Madu River
description: Magical boat journey through Sri Lanka's most biodiverse mangrove forest on the Madu River Lagoon. 64 islands, 303 plant species, monitored crocodiles, and rare birds in a UNESCO-listed wetland.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for bird activity; year-round

name: Ambuluwawa Handicraft Village
description: Traditional handicraft village where artisans demonstrate and sell handmade lacquerware, brassware, woodcarving, cane furniture, and silver jewelry. Authentic Sri Lankan crafts at fair prices.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for demonstrations

name: Vavuniya Archaeology Museum
description: Rarely visited but fascinating regional museum displaying archaeological finds from the Northern Province including Buddhist sculptures, ancient coins, and traditional artifacts from the Chola and Anuradhapura periods.
best_time_to_visit: Weekday mornings

name: Pidurangala Rock (Photography Spot)
description: The lesser-known but better photography viewpoint opposite Sigiriya. A 45-minute hike rewards with iconic sunrise/sunset shots of Sigiriya Rock rising from the jungle canopy. No crowds compared to Sigiriya.
best_time_to_visit: Sunrise (6 AM) for iconic Sigiriya photography

name: Colombo Barefoot Gallery & Café
description: Iconic Colombo institution combining a contemporary art gallery, hand-printed textile shop, bookshop, and garden café. The heart of Colombo's creative and cultural scene for over 40 years.
best_time_to_visit: Any time; weekend afternoon for live music

name: Habarana Village Bicycle Tour
description: Guided bicycle tours through ancient villages in the Cultural Triangle, visiting rural paddy farmers, village temples, tanks (reservoirs), and endemic birdlife. A peaceful authentic cultural experience.
best_time_to_visit: Early morning for cooler temperatures

name: Talangama Wetland Bird Watching
description: Easily accessible urban wetland sanctuary near Colombo city with over 100 bird species including painted storks, purple herons, and kingfishers. Perfect for birdwatching between city sightseeing.
best_time_to_visit: Early morning 6-9 AM for peak bird activity

name: Hikkaduwa Beach (Photography & Nightlife)
description: Lively beach town with colorful beach bars, nightlife, coral snorkeling, and reggae vibes. One of Sri Lanka's most popular backpacker destinations with an active social scene.
best_time_to_visit: November to April; beach parties on weekends

name: Nainativu Nagadeepa Temple
description: Ancient Buddhist and Hindu temple complex on a small island accessible only by boat from Kurikadduwan. One of Sri Lanka's oldest Buddhist sites where the Buddha is believed to have visited twice.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for quieter pilgrimage; full moon days

name: Galle Fort Photography Walk
description: Guided photography walk through the cobblestone streets of Galle Fort with professional photographer guide, covering hidden alleys, colorful doors, lighthouse, cricket ground, and sunset rampart shots.
best_time_to_visit: Golden hour sessions at 16:00 for best light

name: Mulgirigala Rock Temple
description: Ancient Buddhist cave temple complex rising 200m above the jungle on a dramatic granite rock. Seven cave temples with ancient murals and inscriptions dating back to 2nd century BC. Often called the 'mini Sigiriya'.
best_time_to_visit: Early morning

name: Lanka Hydraulic Institute (Model Irrigation)
description: Unique interactive museum showcasing the extraordinary ancient hydraulic civilization of Sri Lanka, featuring scale models of the 2,000-year-old irrigation network that fed an ancient civilisation.
best_time_to_visit: Weekday mornings

name: Negombo Beach & Lagoon
description: Popular beach town just 8km from Colombo Airport perfect for a first/last night stay. Long sandy beach, historic Catholic churches, Dutch canal, and abundant fresh seafood restaurants.
best_time_to_visit: November to April for beach season

name: Bandaranaike International Airport
description: Sri Lanka's main international gateway, located in Katunayake, 30km north of Colombo. Features modern terminals, duty free shopping, currency exchange, SIM card shops, and tourist information.
best_time_to_visit: Arrive 3 hours before departure for international flights

name: Ella Train Journey (Kandy-Ella Scenic Route)
description: Consistently voted one of the world's most beautiful train journeys. The Observation Car (First Class) offers glass-windowed rear viewing through cloud forests, waterfalls, tea estates, and tunnels for 7 hours.
opening_hours: Book via online or station office
best_time_to_visit: Book Observation Car tickets 1-2 weeks in advance

name: Colombo City Tour (Tuk Tuk)
description: Iconic half-day Colombo city tour by three-wheeler (tuk-tuk) visiting Gangaramaya Temple, Viharamahadevi Park, Galle Face Green, Independence Square, Pettah Market, and Old Dutch Hospital.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for cooler temperatures

name: Colombo Viharamahadevi Park
description: Sri Lanka's oldest and largest public park (Victoria Park redesigned) covering 23 acres in the heart of Colombo. Features a boating lake, golden Buddha statue, Japanese garden, and Sunday music concerts.
best_time_to_visit: Weekday morning; Sunday for local atmosphere

name: Dickwella Blow Hole & Hoo-maniya
description: Natural ocean blow hole that can shoot water up to 18 meters into the air during high tide. One of Asia's largest known blow holes, a dramatic natural spectacle on Sri Lanka's southern coast.
opening_hours: Open 24 hours; best during high tide
best_time_to_visit: High tide for maximum water jet; June-July for monsoon surge

name: Jaffna Public Library
description: Rebuilt iconic neoclassical library, a symbol of Tamil cultural resilience after its destruction in 1981. Contains over 100,000 volumes including rare Tamil manuscripts and a significant archive of Tamil literature.
best_time_to_visit: Morning

name: Diyaluma Falls
description: Sri Lanka's second highest waterfall at 220m, accessible via a 2-hour hike through tea estates. Natural rock pools at the top ideal for swimming with spectacular views over Uva province.
best_time_to_visit: Morning; December-April for full water flow

name: Bavarian Lanka Cookie Factory
description: Colonial-era cookie factory in Nuwara Eliya producing traditional shortbread and biscuits using a 100-year-old original German recipe. Factory tour shows traditional baking methods.
best_time_to_visit: Morning baking sessions

name: Katharagama Jungle Safari
description: Off-the-beaten-path jungle safari in the wilderness surrounding Kataragama offering encounters with elephants, leopards, sloth bears, and endemic birds in pristine forest bordering Yala.
best_time_to_visit: May to September dry season

name: Bambarakanda Falls
description: Sri Lanka's highest waterfall at 263m, cascading down the Horton Plains escarpment into the Bambarakanda jungle. Accessible via a short hike and very photogenic in morning light.
best_time_to_visit: December-April when rains feed the falls

name: Koggala Lake Boat Tour
description: Scenic boat tour of Koggala Lake, Sri Lanka's largest natural lake, visiting islands with Buddhist temples, cinnamon plantations, and bird sanctuaries. Watch traditional stilt fishermen along the shoreline.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for bird activity and calm water

name: Wellawaya Buduruwagala Rock Carvings
description: Remarkable 9th-century Mahayana Buddhist rock relief carvings featuring seven colossal figures including a rare standing Tara figure with a flame-like nimbus. A little-visited but extraordinary ancient site.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for best rock carving light

name: Galle Road Stilt Fishermen Photography (Ahangama)
description: Iconic Sri Lanka image - traditional stilt fishermen perched on cross poles in the ocean at Ahangama, a unique ancient fishing method. The most photographed scene in Sri Lanka.
best_time_to_visit: Sunrise for photography; small tip for fishermen expected

name: Marine Drive Colombo Sunset
description: Galle Face Green – the iconic ocean front promenade in Colombo where locals and tourists gather for spectacular Indian Ocean sunsets. Street food vendors, kite flying, and the colonial Galle Face Hotel backdrop.
best_time_to_visit: Sunset hours 17:30-18:30 for spectacular views

name: Jaffna Market & Food Tour
description: Morning food tour through Jaffna's vibrant central market sampling palmyra fruit, dried fish, Northern Tamil sweets, fresh toddy, and authentic Tamil street food. An essential northern Sri Lanka experience.
best_time_to_visit: 7-10 AM for maximum market activity

name: Kelaniya Raja Maha Vihara
description: One of Sri Lanka's most sacred Buddhist temples, 11km from Colombo. Believed to be the spot where the Buddha visited during his third visit to Sri Lanka. Famous for its beautiful murals and Duruthu Perahera.
best_time_to_visit: January Duruthu Perahera; morning or evening puja

name: Wellawatte Textile Market (Colombo)
description: Sri Lanka's best textile and fashion shopping district in Wellawatte. Famous for handloom sarees, batik fabrics, tailoring shops, and multi-ethnic street food. A vibrant multicultural commercial neighborhood.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for fabric shopping; afternoon for street food

name: Aukana Buddha Statue
description: Magnificent 5th-century standing Buddha statue (14.5m tall) carved from a single granite rock. One of the finest examples of ancient Sri Lankan sculpture art, located near the Kala Wewa reservoir.
best_time_to_visit: Sunrise when light hits the statue face beautifully

name: Unawatuna Jungle Beach & Secret Cove
description: Hidden jungle-framed cove accessible via 15-minute coastal hike beyond Unawatuna. Crystal clear waters, dramatic rock formations, and no crowds. One of Sri Lanka's best-kept beach secrets.
best_time_to_visit: November to April; low tide for access

name: Mathalan Mangrove Reserve (Jaffna)
description: Pristine Northern mangrove ecosystem with rare flamingo populations, dugongs, and extraordinary bird diversity rarely visited by international tourists. Offers boat safari through protected channels.
best_time_to_visit: October-March for flamingos and migratory birds

name: Haputale Train Journey & Valley Views
description: The Haputale range offers Sri Lanka's most dramatic valley views with the hill station perched on a ridge overlooking both southern coastal plains and the Uva basin simultaneously.
best_time_to_visit: Clear mornings from June-September; best before 9 AM

name: Canoeing Mahaweli River (Kandy)
description: Gentle river canoeing along Sri Lanka's longest river, the Mahaweli, through jungle and village scenery near Kandy. Calm sections suitable for families and beginners with wildlife spotting.
best_time_to_visit: Morning; November-April for calmer water levels

name: Matara Dutch Fort & Parevi Beach
description: Small but well-preserved Dutch star fort built in 1763 in the center of Matara town, connected to the main fort by a bridge. Adjacent Parevi Beach offers relaxed southern beach town atmosphere.
best_time_to_visit: Morning; November-April for beach

name: Ceylon Tea Museum Kandy
description: Fascinating museum housed in a historic tea factory telling the complete story of Sri Lanka's 150-year-old tea industry. Features original Victorian-era tea machinery, photographs, and interactive exhibits.
best_time_to_visit: Morning

name: Trincomalee Natural Harbour Boat Tour
description: Tour of Trincomalee's famous natural harbour – one of the world's finest and deepest natural harbours with panoramic coastal views, war memorial sites, and whale watching opportunities.
best_time_to_visit: April-September for best conditions

name: Pettah Floating Market Colombo
description: Unique urban floating market concept with vendors selling spices, handicrafts, street food, and fresh produce from boats along a restored Colombo canal. A blend of heritage and modern retail experience.
best_time_to_visit: Evening for lights and atmosphere

name: Gal Oya National Park Boat Safari
description: Sri Lanka's only boat-based national park safari. Canoe through the Senanayake Samudra reservoir (Sri Lanka's largest) spotting swimming elephants, crocodiles, and endemic birds from the water.
best_time_to_visit: May-September dry season for swimming elephants

name: Kumana National Park
description: Premier bird breeding sanctuary with massive seasonal bird colonies including painted storks, ibis, egrets, and herons nesting in large numbers. Adjoins Yala and offers less crowded wildlife viewing.
best_time_to_visit: April-July for nesting colonies; May-September for general wildlife

name: Kekirawa Kandulla National Park
description: Compact national park offering excellent elephant viewing and bird watching around Kandalama and Hurulu tanks. Less visited than Minneriya but often excellent for elephants during June-September.
best_time_to_visit: August-October for elephant congregation

name: Colombo Lotus Tower
description: South Asia's tallest tower at 356m, shaped like a lotus flower. Features observation deck, revolving restaurant, and entertainment complex offering 360-degree views across Colombo and the Indian Ocean.
best_time_to_visit: Sunset or evening for city lights

name: Anuradhapura Mihintale
description: Sacred hill where Buddhism was introduced to Sri Lanka in 247 BC by the Indian missionary Mahinda. Climb 1,840 granite steps to ancient stupas, meditation caves, and the Mahinda Cave.
best_time_to_visit: Morning; June full moon (Poson Poya) festival

name: Dondra Head Lighthouse
description: Sri Lanka's southernmost point and tallest lighthouse (49m). The colonial-era lighthouse marks the southernmost tip of Asia's 'teardrop' with dramatic ocean views and regular whale sighting opportunities offshore.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for whale watching; sunset for photography

name: Point Pedro Northernmost Point
description: Sri Lanka's northernmost point with a historic lighthouse, windswept coastline, and dramatic views to India. A symbolic destination for travelers completing a full island journey from South to North.
best_time_to_visit: Morning; May-September dry season

name: Kalutara Gangatilake Cetiya
description: Unique hollow stupa pagoda where the interior is elaborately decorated with Buddhist murals telling the Jataka stories. The only hollow stupa in Sri Lanka, visible from the Colombo-Galle highway.
best_time_to_visit: Morning

name: Bar Reef Marine Sanctuary (Kalpitiya)
description: Sri Lanka's largest and most diverse coral reef system, home to hawksbill turtles, reef sharks, eagle rays, and hundreds of fish species. One of the most pristine dive sites in South Asia.
best_time_to_visit: December to March for best visibility

name: Colombo Barefoot Sri Lanka Cooking Class
description: Premium cooking class in Colombo's upscale Colombo 7 neighborhood learning traditional Colombo-style Dutch Burgher recipes, devilled dishes, lamprais, and hoppers from a Burgher family descendant.
best_time_to_visit: Any day; advance booking required

name: Hikkaduwa Scuba Diving
description: Popular beginner-to-intermediate scuba diving destination with HMS Hermes British aircraft carrier wreck, coral gardens, moray eels, and sea turtles in 8-25m depth warm water.
best_time_to_visit: November to April for best visibility

name: National Zoological Gardens Dehiwala
description: South Asia's best zoo with 3,500+ animals across 350+ species including leopards, elephants, primates, reptiles, and an excellent aquarium. Set in 25 acres of tropical garden.
best_time_to_visit: Morning; elephant show at 15:30

name: Ella Cutcherry (Tea Estate Walk)
description: Guided tea estate walking tour through Ella's surrounding tea plantations with a local Tamil tea plucker as guide. Learn tea cultivation, hand-plucking, and visit a small estate processing facility.
best_time_to_visit: Morning when pluckers are active

name: Moragaha Meditation Centre
description: Traditional forest meditation retreat offering Vipassana-inspired mindfulness retreats in a jungle setting, with experienced Buddhist meditation masters guiding 3-10 day programs for serious practitioners.
best_time_to_visit: Year-round; pre-registration 2-4 weeks ahead

name: Colombo Night Food Tour
description: Evening guided street food tour exploring Colombo's diverse culinary landscape from hoppers in Slave Island to kottu in Wellawatte, samosa in Pettah, and fresh juices in Fort. Multi-ethnic food experience.
best_time_to_visit: Evening any day of the week

name: Lake Gregory Boating Nuwara Eliya
description: Scenic boating on colonial-era Gregory Lake surrounded by manicured gardens, rose hedges, and misty mountain backdrop. Pedal boats, rowing boats, and speed boat rides available.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for mist photography; April racing season

name: Saptha Vadiya Indigenous Village (Veddah)
description: Authentic cultural encounter with Sri Lanka's indigenous Veddah (Wanniyala-aetto) community. Experience their forest hunting techniques, bow-and-arrow demonstrations, traditional songs, and ancestral lifestyle.
best_time_to_visit: Morning; dry season November-March

name: Colombo Lotus Road Luxury Shopping
description: Colombo's premium shopping street (Galle Road, Colpetty to Bambalapitiya) featuring international designer boutiques, Odel department store, luxury hotels, and upscale restaurants.
best_time_to_visit: Weekdays to avoid weekend crowds

name: Yoda Ela Ancient Irrigation Canal
description: The Yoda Ela (Giant Canal) is a 2nd-century BC masterwork of ancient hydraulic engineering, stretching 87km and still functioning after 2,000 years. A testament to ancient Sri Lankan engineering genius.
best_time_to_visit: Early morning; March-September dry season

name: Debarawewa Tissa Wewa Sunset
description: Ancient reservoir at Tissamaharama offering spectacular sunset views with elephants, waterbirds including painted storks, and the dramatic Hambantota plains backdrop. Often overlooked gem near Yala.
best_time_to_visit: Sunset for spectacular orange sky and elephant silhouettes

name: Kelaniya River Float (Adventure)
description: Relaxed river floating and gentle tubing experience on the Kelani River near Kitulgala-Colombo highway. Easy adventure suitable for non-swimmers with life vests, passing through jungle and village scenery.
best_time_to_visit: Morning; June-October rainy season for better flow

name: Aluvihare Rock Temple (Manuscript Caves)
description: Ancient cave temple where the Pali Buddhist scriptures (Tripitaka) were first written on ola leaf manuscripts in 89 BC. Workshop still practices traditional ola leaf writing. Fascinating for Buddhist scholars.
best_time_to_visit: Morning

name: Colombo Beira Lake Sunset Cruise
description: Evening boat cruise on Colombo's Beira Lake through the heart of the city, passing the Gangaramaya Temple, luxury hotels, and the illuminated Lotus Tower. Cocktails served onboard at sunset.
best_time_to_visit: Sunset hour (17:30-18:30)

name: Hatton Tea Country Scenic Drive
description: Spectacular highland drive through some of the world's most scenic tea country connecting Colombo to Nuwara Eliya via Hatton. Rolling green tea carpets, waterfalls, and mountain tunnels.
opening_hours: Road open 24 hours; best in daylight
best_time_to_visit: Morning for mist-free views; January-March

name: Colombo Colombo7 Art Gallery District
description: Colombo's most vibrant art and gallery district in Colombo 7 featuring contemporary Sri Lankan art galleries, indie cafés, art studios, and design boutiques. Hosts First Thursday gallery nights monthly.
best_time_to_visit: First Thursday evening for art walk events

name: Negombo St. Mary's Church
description: Magnificent neo-Gothic Catholic church reflecting Negombo's strong Catholic heritage from Portuguese colonial period. Stunning stained glass, elaborate altar paintings depicting Sri Lankan scenes in Christian context.
best_time_to_visit: Sunday morning Mass for full cultural experience

name: Hambantota Salt Pans
description: Traditional salt evaporation pans stretching across the Hambantota lagoon operated by salt farmers for centuries. The geometric pink-white salt flats create extraordinary photography opportunities, especially at sunrise.
best_time_to_visit: Sunrise for pink-tinted salt and bird photography; dry season

name: Puttalam Lagoon Prawn Farm Tour
description: Sri Lanka's largest prawn and shrimp aquaculture region. Guided tours of commercial prawn farms with fresh prawn tasting, cooking demonstrations, and insight into Sri Lanka's seafood export industry.
best_time_to_visit: Year-round; harvest season May-October

name: Colombo National Aquarium
description: Modern aquarium in Colombo's Mattakkuliya area showcasing Sri Lanka's extraordinary marine biodiversity including sharks, rays, sea turtles, and colorful reef fish from Sri Lankan and global waters.
best_time_to_visit: Weekday morning for smaller crowds

name: Kandy Gem Bureau & Market
description: Authentic gem trading center in Kandy offering certified blue sapphires, rubies, alexandrite, and cat's eyes directly from Sri Lankan mines at transparent prices with government certification available.
best_time_to_visit: Morning for trader activity

name: Mullaitivu War Memorial & Lagoon
description: Significant site of contemporary Sri Lankan history on the northeastern coast. A sober war memorial and pristine natural lagoon where fishing communities are rebuilding. Important for understanding modern Sri Lanka.
best_time_to_visit: May-September dry season

name: Meemure Village (Last Ancient Village)
description: Remote village in the Knuckles wilderness considered one of Sri Lanka's last truly traditional villages, accessible only on foot or by 4WD. No mobile signal, electricity until recently. Homestay available.
best_time_to_visit: January to April and August-September; dry season

name: Tangalle Blow Hole & Rekawa Circuit
description: Southern coast natural circuit combining the Tangalle area's dramatic blow hole at Dickwella, turtle nesting beach at Rekawa, and the secluded bays between Tangalle town and Hambantota.
best_time_to_visit: December to April; turtle season February-August
